claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm inquiring about - my son Alex. He's 16 years old and lives with me. I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 16 years old and lives with you.

Since Alex is 16, there are specific rules that apply. You can normally still qualify for Child Benefit for a child aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as school, college, or an approved training program)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. Alex is studying for his A-levels at college right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great! Since Alex is 16, living with you, and in approved education (studying for his A-levels), that's a positive indicator for eligibility.

Now I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. One final question to complete my assessment:

**Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, do you live with him and/or pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit would be (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after him - for example on food, clothes or pocket money?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. He lives with me and I'm responsible for all his needs - food, clothes, everything really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 16 and in approved education (studying for A-levels at college) - children aged 16-19 qualify if they stay in approved education or training

✓ You live in the UK

✓ You are responsible for Alex - he lives with you and you provide for all his needs

✓ Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You meet all the requirements for Child Benefit. You can continue to receive Child Benefit for Alex as long as he remains in approved education or training and is under 20 years old.

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can still claim Child Benefit, but you might have to pay some or all of it back through tax.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit. If Alex's circumstances change (such as leaving education or starting work for 24+ hours a week), you'll need to inform HMRC.

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. The claimant meets all the required criteria: (1) Alex is 16 years old and staying in approved education (studying for A-levels at college), which means he qualifies under the 16-19 rule for children in approved education or training; (2) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester); (3) The claimant is responsible for Alex - he lives with them and they provide for all his needs including food and clothes; (4) Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility requirements are satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 16-year-old son Alex. Alex meets the age criteria as he is in approved education (A-levels at college), the claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex who lives with them, and no one else is claiming for him.

All eligibility requirements are satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 16-year-old son Alex. Alex meets the age criteria as he is in approved education (A-levels at college), the claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex who lives with them, and no one else is claiming for him.
